info.description: Midaz Tracer API — pre-flight transaction validation. Provides CEL-based rule evaluation, spending limits, two-phase reservations (hold / confirm / release), validation decisions, and a hash-chained audit trail.
info.title: Midaz Tracer API

get.description: Returns 503 until startup self-probe completes; 200 "healthy" thereafter.
Live dependency probes — never cached. Returns 200 only when every check is up/skipped/n/a; any down or degraded forces 503.
Lists audit events with filters and cursor-based pagination. SOX/GLBA compliance endpoint.
Retrieves a single audit event by its unique identifier. SOX/GLBA compliance endpoint.
get.summary: Get an audit event by ID

get.description: Verifies the integrity of the audit event hash chain up to a specific event. Detects tampering attempts. SOX/GLBA compliance endpoint.
get.summary: Verify audit event hash chain integrity

parameters.description: Event ID to verify up to (UUID)
parameters.name: id

get.description: Lists limits with cursor-based pagination and optional filters.

parameters.description: Filter by scope sub_type (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase; max 50 chars)
parameters.name: sub_type

post.description: Creates a limit with scopes array. Limits are created in DRAFT status.

get.description: Retrieves a limit by its unique identifier.
get.summary: Get a spending limit by ID

delete.description: Soft-deletes a limit (transitions to DELETED status). Only DRAFT and INACTIVE limits can be deleted. ACTIVE limits must be deactivated first.

patch.description: Partially updates a limit. Only provided fields are updated, omitted fields remain unchanged.
patch.summary: Partially update an existing spending limit

post.description: Transitions a limit from INACTIVE to DRAFT status. Allows re-editing a previously deactivated limit.
post.summary: Transition a limit back to draft

get.description: Retrieves current usage snapshot for a limit, showing aggregated usage, utilization percentage, and reset time.
get.summary: Get usage snapshot for a limit

post.description: Holds limit capacity for a ledger transaction without committing it. Returns one reservation id per counter-backed limit, or denied=true when a limit would be exceeded.

Confirms EVERY held reservation a transaction holds, addressed by the ledger transaction id. The ledger /commit drives this with only the transaction id. Idempotent — flipped=0 (no reservations, or all already terminal) returns 200.
post.summary: Confirm a transaction's reservations (phase two — commit by transaction)

post.description: Releases EVERY held reservation a transaction holds, addressed by the ledger transaction id. The ledger /cancel drives this with only the transaction id. Idempotent — flipped=0 returns 200.
post.summary: Release a transaction's reservations (phase two — abort by transaction)

post.description: Commits a held reservation: the amount moves from reserved to current usage. Idempotent — a retry against an already-terminal reservation returns 200.
Returns a held reservation's capacity on an aborted ledger transaction. Idempotent — a retry against an already-terminal reservation returns 200.

get.description: Lists rules with cursor-based pagination and optional filters. Supports filtering by scope fields to find rules applicable to specific contexts. Global rules (empty scopes) are always included in scope-filtered results.

parameters.description: Filter by scope sub_type (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase; max 50 chars)
parameters.name: sub_type

post.description: Creates a rule with CEL expression and scopes array. Rules are created in DRAFT status.

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. expression cost limit exceeded)

get.description: Retrieves a rule by its unique identifier.
get.summary: Get a fraud rule by ID

delete.description: Soft-deletes a rule (transitions to DELETED status). Only DRAFT and INACTIVE rules can be deleted. ACTIVE rules must be deactivated first.

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. invalid status transition — active rules must be deactivated first)

patch.description: Partially updates a rule. Only provided fields are updated, omitted fields remain unchanged.
patch.summary: Partially update an existing fraud rule

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. expression cost limit exceeded or expression not modifiable)

post.description: Activates a rule (DRAFT/INACTIVE → ACTIVE). Validates CEL expression before activation.

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. invalid status transition or expression cost exceeded)
Business rule violation (e.g. invalid status transition)

post.description: Transitions a rule from INACTIVE to DRAFT status. Allows re-editing a previously deactivated rule.
post.summary: Transition a rule back to draft

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. invalid status transition)

get.description: Lists transaction validation records with cursor-based pagination and filters.

post.description: Validates a transaction against configured rules and limits.

422.description: Business rule violation (e.g. amount exceeds safe precision)

get.description: Retrieves a transaction validation record by its unique identifier.
get.summary: Get a transaction validation record by ID

get.description: Returns the current version of the service plus build provenance

AuditEvent.description: Immutable audit record for SOX/GLBA compliance. Each event captures who performed what action on which resource, the outcome, and a full context snapshot. Events are hash-chained to detect tampering; the hash covers all core fields.

context.description:
Context (JSONB): for validations: { request: {...}, response: {reason, processingTimeMs, matchedRuleIds, ...} }; for CRUD: { before: {...}, after: {...}, reason: "..." }
Note: accountId, segmentId, portfolioId are in context.request.account (not first-level fields)

createdAt.description:
Timestamp when the event occurred
format: date-time

eventId.description:
Unique identifier for this audit event
format: uuid

eventType.description:
Type of event that occurred
example: TRANSACTION_VALIDATED
enums: TRANSACTION_VALIDATED,RULE_CREATED,RULE_UPDATED,RULE_ACTIVATED,RULE_DEACTIVATED,RULE_DRAFTED,RULE_DELETED,LIMIT_CREATED,LIMIT_UPDATED,LIMIT_DELETED,LIMIT_ACTIVATED,LIMIT_DEACTIVATED,LIMIT_DRAFTED,RESERVATION_RESERVED,RESERVATION_CONFIRMED,RESERVATION_RELEASED,RESERVATION_EXPIRED,RESERVATION_SKIPPED

hash.description:
SHA-256 hash of this event's fields for tamper detection
example: a3f1e2b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2

metadata.description: Additional metadata such as ticketId or correlationId

previousHash.description:
Hash of the preceding event in the chain, empty for the first event
example: b4e2f3a5c6d7e8f9b0c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3

resourceId.description:
ID of the resource affected by this event
example: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000

resourceType.description:
Type of resource affected
example: transaction
enums: transaction,rule,limit,reservation

result.description:
Outcome: ALLOW/DENY/REVIEW for validations; SUCCESS/FAILED for CRUD operations
example: ALLOW
enums: ALLOW,DENY,REVIEW,SUCCESS,FAILED

Limit.description: A transaction spending limit enforced during validation. Limits accumulate usage across a configurable period (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/CUSTOM/PER_TRANSACTION) and block or flag transactions that would exceed the configured MaxAmount for the matched currency and scope.

activeTimeEnd.description: End of the daily time window when the limit is active (HH:MM), null means 24/7

activeTimeStart.description: Start of the daily time window when the limit is active (HH:MM), null means 24/7

createdAt.description:
Timestamp when the limit was created
format: date-time

currency.description:
ISO 4217 currency code this limit applies to
example: USD

customEndDate.description:
End of custom period (only for CUSTOM limitType)
format: date-time

customStartDate.description:
Start of custom period (only for CUSTOM limitType)
format: date-time

deletedAt.description:
Timestamp when the limit was soft deleted, null if not deleted
format: date-time

description.description:
Optional description of the limit's purpose
example: Caps daily USD outflows for retail accounts
description.example: Caps daily USD outflows for retail accounts

limitId.description:
Unique identifier for the limit
format: uuid

limitType.description:
Period type for usage accumulation
enums: DAILY,WEEKLY,MONTHLY,CUSTOM,PER_TRANSACTION

maxAmount.description: Maximum amount allowed within the period

name.description:
Human-readable name of the limit
example: Daily USD spending cap
maxLength: 255

resetAt.description:
Next reset timestamp, null for PER_TRANSACTION limits
format: date-time

scopes.description: Scopes that restrict which transactions this limit applies to

status.description:
Current lifecycle status of the limit
enums: DRAFT,ACTIVE,INACTIVE,DELETED

updatedAt.description:
Timestamp when the limit was last updated
format: date-time

attemptedAmount.description:
AttemptedAmount is the transaction amount being validated against this limit.
Matches the amount from the validation request.

currentUsage.description:
CurrentUsage represents the PROJECTED usage after applying the transaction amount,
not the actual persisted counter value. This is calculated as:
(counter.CurrentUsage + input.Amount) for DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/CUSTOM limits, or 0 for PER_TRANSACTION.
When Exceeded=true, the counter was NOT incremented, but CurrentUsage still shows
what the usage would have been if the transaction were allowed.

period.description: Period indicates the type of limit (DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, CUSTOM, PER_TRANSACTION).

scope.description:
Scope is a human-readable string representation of the limit's scope
(e.g., "account:uuid" or "segment:uuid" or "global").

skipReason.description:
SkipReason explains why the limit was skipped (only set when Skipped=true).
Values: "outside_time_window" (outside active hours), "outside_custom_period" (outside custom date range).

skipped.description:
Skipped indicates whether this limit was skipped during evaluation (not enforced).
When true, the counter was NOT incremented and Exceeded is always false.

longLived.description:
LongLived selects the reservation lifetime. false (the default, a direct
transaction) gets the short reaper-swept TTL; true (a PENDING transaction)
gets the long-lived TTL so the reservation does not expire under a
still-valid pending that has no existing sweep (R18). It is a sibling wire
field, NOT part of the embedded ValidationRequest, so the relaxed reserve
validation never sees it.

transactionId.description:
TransactionID is the ledger transaction correlation id. It is the
idempotency grain for retried reserves and the handle the ledger later
confirms or releases. Not a foreign key — the ledger transaction lives in a
different service.

Rule.description: A validation rule containing a CEL expression evaluated against transaction context. Rules are created in DRAFT status, activated to apply enforcement, and produce a Decision (ALLOW/DENY/REVIEW) when matched.

action.description:
Decision produced when this rule matches
enums: ALLOW,DENY,REVIEW

activatedAt.description:
Timestamp when the rule was last activated, null if never activated
format: date-time

createdAt.description:
Timestamp when the rule was created
format: date-time

deactivatedAt.description:
Timestamp when the rule was last deactivated, null if currently active or never deactivated
format: date-time

deletedAt.description:
Timestamp when the rule was soft deleted, null if not deleted
format: date-time

description.description:
Optional description of the rule's purpose
example: Denies transactions over $1000 from checking accounts
description.example: Denies transactions over $1000 from checking accounts

expression.description:
CEL expression evaluated against transaction context
example: transaction.amount > 1000 && account.type == 'checking'

name.description:
Human-readable name of the rule
example: Block high-value checking transactions
maxLength: 255

ruleId.description:
Unique identifier for the rule
format: uuid

scopes.description: Scopes that restrict which transactions this rule applies to

status.description:
Current lifecycle status of the rule
enums: DRAFT,ACTIVE,INACTIVE,DELETED

updatedAt.description:
Timestamp when the rule was last updated
format: date-time

Scope.description: Hierarchical scope that constrains which transactions a rule or limit applies to. At least one field must be set. When multiple fields are set, all must match (AND semantics). Nil fields match any value.

accountId.description:
Account the scope is restricted to (optional)
format: uuid

merchantId.description:
Merchant the scope is restricted to (optional)
format: uuid

portfolioId.description:
Portfolio the scope is restricted to (optional)
format: uuid

segmentId.description:
Segment the scope is restricted to (optional)
format: uuid

subType.description:
SubType is normalized to lowercase canonical form; matching is case-insensitive.
example: purchase
maxLength: 50

transactionType.description:
Transaction type the scope is restricted to (optional)
example: CARD

TransactionValidation.description: Persisted record of a completed transaction validation, combining the original request context with the evaluation outcome. Used for compliance auditing, idempotency replay, and historical querying. Embeds EvaluationResult fields (decision, reason, matchedRuleIds, etc.) directly.

account.description: Account context from the validation request

createdAt.description:
Timestamp when this validation record was created
format: date-time

currency.description:
ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction
example: USD

decision.description:
Final decision produced by rule evaluation
enums: ALLOW,DENY,REVIEW

evaluatedRuleIds.description: IDs of all rules evaluated during this request

limitUsageDetails.description: Per-limit usage details captured at validation time

matchedRuleIds.description: IDs of rules that matched the transaction scope and CEL expression

merchant.description: Merchant context from the validation request (optional)

metadata.description: Additional metadata from the validation request

portfolio.description: Portfolio context from the validation request (optional)

processingTimeMs.description:
Time taken to evaluate rules and limits in milliseconds
example: 12.5

reason.description:
Human-readable explanation of the decision
example: Transaction denied by rule 'Block high-value checking transactions'
reason.example: Transaction denied by rule 'Block high-value checking transactions'

requestId.description:
Idempotency key from the originating validation request
format: uuid

segment.description: Segment context from the validation request (optional)

subType.description:
SubType is stored in its lowercase canonical form; matching is case-insensitive.
example: purchase
maxLength: 50

totalRulesLoaded.description:
Total number of active rules loaded for evaluation before any truncation
example: 42

transactionTimestamp.description:
Timestamp of the original transaction
format: date-time

transactionType.description:
Type of the transaction that was validated
example: CARD

truncated.description:
True when active rules exceeded MAX_RULES_PER_REQUEST and were truncated
example: false

validationId.description:
Unique identifier for this validation record
format: uuid

currentUsage.description: Current usage amount (sum of all counters)

subType.description: SubType is normalized to lowercase canonical form; matching is case-insensitive.

ValidationResponse.description: Response returned after evaluating a transaction against the active rules and limits. Contains the evaluation decision, matched rule identifiers, per-limit usage details, and timing metadata. EvaluationResult fields (decision, reason, matchedRuleIds, evaluatedRuleIds, totalRulesLoaded, truncated) are promoted to the top level via Go embedding.

decision.description:
Final decision produced by rule evaluation
enums: ALLOW,DENY,REVIEW

evaluatedAt.description:
Timestamp when the evaluation was performed
format: date-time

evaluatedRuleIds.description: IDs of all rules evaluated during this request

limitUsageDetails.description: Per-limit usage details for every limit checked during this validation

matchedRuleIds.description: IDs of rules that matched the transaction scope and CEL expression

processingTimeMs.description:
Time taken to evaluate rules and limits in milliseconds
example: 12.5

reason.description:
Human-readable explanation of the decision
example: Transaction denied by rule 'Block high-value checking transactions'
reason.example: Transaction denied by rule 'Block high-value checking transactions'

requestId.description:
Idempotency key echoed from the request
format: uuid

totalRulesLoaded.description:
Total number of active rules loaded for evaluation before any truncation
example: 42

truncated.description:
True when active rules exceeded MAX_RULES_PER_REQUEST and were truncated
example: false

validationId.description:
Unique identifier for this validation result
format: uuid

tags.description: Two-phase balance reservations: hold, then confirm or release.
tags.name: Reservations
